title: Random Pick with Weight
problem_statement: Design a function that randomly picks an index from an array with probability proportional to its weight.

examples.explanation: Index 1 is three times more likely to be chosen because its weight is three times larger.

intuition: Build a prefix sum array and use binary search on a random target to choose an index with probability proportional to weight.

approaches.name: Prefix Sum with Binary Search
approaches.description: Accumulate weights into a prefix sum array, generate a random number in [1, totalWeight], and binary search for the corresponding index.
approaches.why_not: Repeatedly sampling from the original array would be too slow for large weight sums.

interview_qa.q: Why use a prefix sum array?
interview_qa.a: It converts weighted probabilities into contiguous intervals so a single random sample can choose an index efficiently.
Binary search locates the interval that contains the random target in O(log n) time.
